A BRIEF, WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE EARLY EVENING JUST TO THE NORTHWEST OF CUBA, MISSOURI. THE TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED DOWN TO THE WEST OF INDIAN LAKE, ALONG LAKESHORE DRIVE. THE TORNADO DID SOME MINOR DAMAGE TO A ROOF ON THE WEST SIDE OF LAKESHORE DRIVE. THE WORST OF THE DAMAGE OCCURRED TO TWO RESIDENCES ON THE EAST SIDE OF LAKESHORE DRIVE, ON THE WEST SIDE OF INDIAN LAKE. A FEW TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED, AND A HOME LOST THE MAJORITY OF ITS ROOF. ADDITIONALLY, A BOAT DOCK WAS COMPLETELY FLIPPED OVER. THE TORNADO MOVED EAST ACROSS THE LAKE, SNAPPING A FEW OTHER TREES AND DOING SOME MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOME HOME ALONG INDIAN HILLS DRIVE. THE TORNADO QUICKLY LIFTED JUST BEFORE IT HIT SEMINOLE ROAD.

A TORNADO RAPIDLY DEVELOPED SATURDAY EVENING NEAR HILLSBORO ROAD TO THE EAST OF PARK HILLS, MISSOURI. THIS TORNADO INITIALLY CAUSED SPORADIC TREE DAMAGE TO A GROVE OF TREES WEST OF HILLSBORO ROAD. AS IT APPROACHED HILLSBORO ROAD, THE TORNADO WIDENED AND BECAME A BIT STRONGER, CAUSING EF-1 DAMAGE TO A HOME BY COLLAPSING ITS SOUTHWARD FACING EXTERIOR WALL. MINOR ROOF DAMAGE ALSO OCCURRED TO ANOTHER HOME, ALONG WITH SEVERAL TRESS THAT WERE SNAPPED. THE TORNADO THEN CROSSED HILLSBORO ROAD AND DID SOME MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO AN OUTBUILDING ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE ROADWAY. THE TORNADO LIFTED AS IT APPROACHED A LARGE GROVE OF TREES ON THE EASTERN SIDE OF HILLSBORO ROAD.

TWO SPOTTERS REPORTED A TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN IN A FIELD VERIFIED WITH A PICTURE. THIS TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NORTHEAST OF HETTICK, IL, SOUTHWEST OF OTTER CREEK. THE TORNADO WAS VERY SHORT LIVED AND DID NOT DAMAGE ANY RATING INDICATORS, THUS IT WILL BE LABELED AN EF-UNKNOWN.
TREE DOWN ACROSS ABBEY LN. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

A COMBINATION OF A GROUND SURVEY AND SATELLITE DATA SHOWS AN INTERMITTENT, 3.2 MILE TORNADO PATH THAT BEGAN JUST SOUTH OF PRANGE AVENUE. IT SNAPPED AND UPROOTED SOME TREES NEAR A RESIDENCE NORTH OF PRANGE AVENUE. SATELLITE DATA SHOWS THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST THROUGH CORNFIELDS, DAMAGING MORE TREES NEAR SEWING AND MT. OLIVE TRAILS. SATELLITE DATA SHOWS THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST BEFORE LIFTING JUST WEST OF NIEMANVILLE TRAIL.

MULTIPLE TREES DOWN, ROOFS REMOVED OR DAMAGED, AND POWER LINES DOWN.

A COMBINATION OF A GROUND SURVEY AND SATELLITE DATA SUGGESTS A BRIEF, WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NORTHEAST OF WALSHVILLE, IL. THE TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED DOWN IN A CORN FIELD WEST OF DURBIN ROAD. THE TORNADO THEN CONTINUED NORTHEAST, MAINLY CAUSING TREE DAMAGE, ALTHOUGH SOME MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO A HOUSE WEST OF DURBIN ROAD ALSO OCCURRED. THE TORNADO QUICKLY LIFTED AS IT APPROACHED DURBIN ROAD.

A TORNADO DEVELOPED ABOUT 3 MILES NORTH-NORTHWEST OF FARMERSVILLE, ILLINOIS NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF ROVEY LANE AND EAST 1ST ROAD. A FEW STORM CHASERS CAPTURED VIDEO OF THE TORNADO AS IT MOVED NORTHEAST AND THEN NORTH TOWARD THE MONTGOMERY AND SANGAMON COUNTY LINE. ON VIDEO THE TORNADO APPEARED TO BE MULTIVORTEX AND VIOLENT, YET DAMAGE IN THE ST. LOUIS CWA WAS LIMITED TO A FEW BROKEN TREE BRANCHES, FLATTENED CORN AND THE PARTIAL DESTRUCTION OF A SMALL OUTBUILDING.
